headline: Shift Grid Costs to Large Energy Users
short_summary: This bill aims to shift the cost of grid infrastructure, such as distribution lines and capacity auction costs, from residential ratepayers to large energy consumers, primarily data centers.
status: Last Action: Governor's recommendation received by House on 2026-04-13
supporters: Consumer advocates, environmental groups, Senator L. Louise Lucas, Del. Destiny LeVere Bolling.
opponents: Dominion Energy (regarding some amendments), data centers/large industrial users.
news_headline: Governor Amends Bills Shifting Data Center Costs
news_summary: Governor Abigail Spanberger's amendments to HB 1393 and SB 253 aim to save money for residential customers by restricting Dominion's spending on line undergrounding and capping its return on equity. Critics argue these tweaks might weaken the original intent of shifting costs to data centers.

headline: Mandate Data Center Water Use Reporting
short_summary: This bill requires water users providing water to data centers to report monthly or quarterly water consumption, including reclaimed water, to the State Water Control Board, enhancing transparency on resource use.
status: Last Action: Approved by Governor-Chapter 896 (Effective 1/1/2027) on 2026-04-13
supporters: Environmental groups, communities, Senator Kannan Srinivasan, Del. Liz Guzman.
news_headline: Data Center Water Use Reporting Becomes Law
news_summary: SB 553, now approved by the Governor, mandates utilities to report data center water consumption, providing communities with clearer insight into local resource use. This is seen as a moderate step towards regulating the rapidly growing tech facilities.

headline: Regulate Data Center Siting and Noise
short_summary: This bill requires site assessments for new high energy use facilities, including data centers, to examine noise impacts on nearby residential areas and schools, and allows localities to assess impacts on water, agriculture, parks, historic sites, and forestland.
supporters: Local communities, residents, environmental groups, Delegate Josh Thomas, Senator Danica Roem (for similar SB 94).
news_headline: New Laws Address Data Center Siting, Noise
news_summary: HB 153, now law, introduces new siting requirements for large data centers, mandating impact assessments on noise, agriculture, parks, and historical sites before rezoning approval. This legislation aims to address community concerns about data center development.
